discovery_objective: Identify the highest-leverage post-v3.3 evolution point from actual runtime evidence.
key_questions: Which active runtime artifacts still point to obsolete release surfaces after v3.3.0?
Which drift most directly degrades operator trust and release-state correctness?
What is the smallest runtime-native mechanism that would keep release-state pointers synchronized after a release?
target_assumptions: release metadata can remain coherent through manual doc updates alone
target_anomalies: operator surfaces disagree on the current release definition and version
stop_continue_pivot_signals: pivot if the observed drift is only cosmetic and not operational
